payload.text:
Without compromising the present features of this application, can you analyze the following prompt and modify the application to have the following functionality in this prompt below;


SYSTEM ROLE & MINDSET
You are a senior full-stack engineer, software architect, and product designer with deep understanding of:
• Nigerian real estate law (Land Use Act, state tenancy laws, Governor’s Consent regime)
• Agent-based property markets (Nigeria/Africa)
• Commission-driven platforms
• Compliance-aware fintech/proptech systems
You must think in systems, workflows, incentives, and auditability, not just UI.
🎯 APPLICATION GOAL
Build a legally intelligent, agent-first real estate management and transaction platform for Nigeria that:
• Does NOT replace property agents
• Protects and automates agent commissions
• Embeds Nigerian real estate law into workflows
• Reduces fraud, disputes, and document risk
• Scales across Nigerian states with state-specific legal logic
Agents must earn more by using the platform than by bypassing it.
👥 CORE USER ROLES (ROLE-BASED ACCESS IS MANDATORY)
• Property Agent (Primary User)
• Property Manager
• Landlord
• Tenant / Buyer
• Platform Admin / Compliance Officer
Each role must have distinct permissions, screens, and workflows.
🧠 CORE ARCHITECTURAL REQUIREMENTS
Design the system using a modular, service-oriented architecture with the following major subsystems:
1️⃣ USER & IDENTITY SERVICE
• Secure authentication (JWT/OAuth)
• Role-based access control
• Agent verification status
• Upgrade path (e.g. Tenant → Agent)
• Audit trail of role changes
Agents must be verified before earning commissions.
2️⃣ PROPERTY MANAGEMENT SERVICE
• Property onboarding (agent-led)
• Mandatory agent assignment per property
• Property metadata: 
• State
• Property type
• Use (residential/commercial)
• Transaction type (lease, sale, sublease)
• Immutable history (who uploaded, edited, reassigned)
No property may go live without an assigned agent or property manager.
3️⃣ LEGAL INTELLIGENCE ENGINE (RULE-BASED, NOT GUESSING)
Embed Nigerian real estate law into logic.
Design a rules engine that:
• Accepts inputs: 
• State
• Transaction type
• Property type
• Available documents
• Land tenure (statutory/customary)
• Applies deterministic rules such as: 
• Governor’s Consent requirement
• State tenancy law checks
• Customary land risk flags
• Outputs: 
• Legal completeness score
• Risk warnings
• Required documents checklist
• Agent guidance notes
⚠️ This engine guides — it does not draft legal documents.
4️⃣ TRANSACTION & LEASING WORKFLOW SERVICE
Support:
• Property inspection scheduling
• Offer → acceptance flow
• Lease lifecycle tracking
• Sale transaction tracking
• Milestone-based progress tracking
All communication must be agent-routed, not direct landlord-tenant bypass.
5️⃣ COMMISSION & INCENTIVE ENGINE (CRITICAL)
Design a deterministic, transparent commission system.
Must support:
• Leasing commissions
• Sales commissions
• Property management fees
• Referral commissions
• Renewal commissions
Required Mechanics:
• Commission locking at client introduction
• Timestamped referral chains
• Automatic commission splitting
• Escrow-based holding
• Milestone-based release
• Agent wallets with payout history
Agents must be able to verify how every kobo was calculated.
6️⃣ REFERRAL & NETWORK ECONOMY
Agents can:
• Refer tenants
• Refer buyers
• Refer landlords
• Refer other agents
System must:
• Track referral origin
• Prevent hijacking
• Reward network builders
• Support multi-level (but capped) referral logic
7️⃣ AGENT REPUTATION & PERFORMANCE SYSTEM
Design a reputation engine using:
• Deal completion rate
• Compliance score
• Client ratings
• Dispute frequency
Reputation affects:
• Listing priority
• Referral commission %
• Visibility in searches
• Access to premium opportunities
8️⃣ DISPUTE PREVENTION & AUDIT SYSTEM
Implement:
• Immutable activity logs
• Timestamped communications
• Inspection records
• Document hash verification
• Transaction history replay
Goal: prevent court cases, not escalate them.
9️⃣ ADMIN & COMPLIANCE DASHBOARD
Admins can:
• Resolve commission disputes
• Review flagged properties
• Suspend bad actors
• Adjust system rules (not transactions)
• Monitor state-specific compliance metrics
💰 AGENT INCENTIVE DESIGN (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
Ensure agents:
• Earn multiple income streams
• Benefit from renewals
• Gain long-term reputation value
• Are protected from landlord/tenant bypass
Design:
• Agent tiers (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Elite)
• Commission multipliers
• Territorial or specialization advantages
• Automatic penalties for bad behavior
🗂️ DATA & STORAGE REQUIREMENTS
• Relational DB (transactions, commissions, users)
• NoSQL (logs, chats, notifications)
• Secure object storage (documents)
• Event sourcing for: 
• Commission events
• Property assignment events
• Referral events
🔐 SECURITY & COMPLIANCE
• Full audit trails
• Tamper-proof logs
• Role-restricted endpoints
• Transaction integrity checks
Design with legal defensibility in mind.
🎨 UI / UX REQUIREMENTS
• Professional, minimalist, trust-focused design
• Agent-first dashboards
• Clear transaction timelines
• Transparent commission views
• Legal risk indicators (green / amber / red)
🚀 OUTPUT EXPECTATION
Generate:
• System architecture (frontend + backend)
• Database schema
• Core API endpoints
• Key workflows (agent onboarding, property listing, leasing, commission payout)
• Sample pseudocode for: 
• Legal rules engine
• Commission locking logic
• Scalable foundation suitable for Nigeria first, Africa later
❗ IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS
• Do NOT remove agents from the system
• Do NOT allow direct landlord-tenant bypass
• Do NOT oversimplify Nigerian legal realities
• Do NOT assume Western real estate models
FINAL INSTRUCTION
Build this as real infrastructure, not a demo.
Think like the system will be audited, disputed, and scaled.
Optimize for trust, legality, and agent prosperity.


Make sure data flow is structured well and the present application structure isn't disrupted

**Developing Core Concepts**
